subject: LU-17460 lnet: support IPv6 for link state

values.-1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this
values.+1: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve

Patch Set 4:

> I can easily test for TCP IPv6. IB is a bit more difficult. I have
 > an IB system but no IPv6 setup on it.

Not sure which procedure you're planning to use, but in the past I 
found the important steps to do when testing this feature are:

1) Test both "soft" and "hard" link disruption (i.e. "ip link set" vs. "pull cable out with your hand"). "Hard" disconnect may probably also be simulated by disabling a port on the switch.
2) Test what happens when originally assigned IP is deleted.
3) Test using a multi-port card with more than one interface used by LNet.
4) Test using bonded interfaces

The reason why I'm mentioning this is because I remember that skipping these steps for the new changes in the related area can turn into an issue.
I'll create a wiki which describes the procedure more coherently.
I'll also check if I can do anything with IPv6 IB on my setup.

commit.subject: LU-17460 lnet: support IPv6 for link state
commit.message:
LU-17460 lnet: support IPv6 for link state

The LNet layer montiors the state of the underlying TCP
connection. Currently it only supports network interfaces
setup with IPv4 addresses. Update to handle IPv6 setups.

Test-Parameters: trivial testlist=sanity-lnet
Change-Id: I249e9591d5f637112f6bd862cd0f928a555af229
Signed-off-by: James Simmons <jsimmons@infradead.org>
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/53761
Reviewed-by: Serguei Smirnov <ssmirnov@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Frank Sehr <fsehr@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>
Tested-by: jenkins <devops@whamcloud.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <maloo@whamcloud.com>
